LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SANDY LIM'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT AMERICAN GENERAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE FOR DEPOSITION AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. AMERICAN GENERAL, AT PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST, PROPERLY DESIGNATED ANGELA BUTLER AS THE PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE TO TESTIFY ON ITS BEHALF IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S PMK NOTICE. IT DID NOT REFUSE TO MAKE MS. BUTLER AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION, BUT RATHER WAS FORCED TO RESCHEDULE HER DEPOSITION DUE TO A PERSONAL FAMILY EMERGENCY ON HER PART. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED IN CAMERA MS. BUTLER'S DECLARATION, WHICH SUBSTANTIATES DEFENDANT'S POSITION. IT APPEARS TO THE COURT FROM THE INFORMATION SUBMITTED TO IT IN CAMERA THAT AMERICAN GENERAL WILL NOT BE IN A POSITION TO MAKE MS. BUTLER AVAILABLE FOR A PMK DEPOSITION BEFORE APRIL 26 AT THE EARLIEST. AMERICAN GENERAL SHOULD BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS WHETHER IT INTENDS TO DESIGNATE AN ALTERNATIVE PMK WITNESS AND, IF SO, WHEN THAT PERSON'S DEPOSITION MAY BE TAKEN IN SAN FRANCISCO, AS REQUIRED BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2025.250(B). IN ADDITION, THE PARTIES SHOULD BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS WHETHER ANY CONTINUANCE OF THE EXISTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT SCHEDULE AND TRIAL DATE WILL BE REQUIRED. DEFENDANT AMERICAN GENERAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY SHALL DESIGNATE A PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE TO TESTIFY ON THE REMAINING TOPICS OF THE DEPOSITION NOTICE AND SHALL MAKE THAT PERSON AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION IN SAN FRANCISCO PRIOR TO MAY 31, 2019. COUNSEL SHALL MEET AND CONFER WITH RESPECT TO ANY OTHER OUTSTANDING DISPUTES REGARDING DISCOVERY REQUESTS APPENDED TO THE PMK NOTICE. NICHOLAS J. BOSS, ESQ. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT AMERICAN GENERAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY IS TO SUBMIT AN ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600 EMAIL MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Second Motion To Compel Defendant American General Life Insurance Companys Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Defendant American General Life Insurance Companys Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Text Ruling

Plaintiff Sandy Lim'S Second Motion To Compel Defendant American General Life Insurance Companys Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Terrence J. coleman, Esq., Pillsbury & Coleman, appearing for Plaintiff;
Nicholas J. Boss, Esq., Maynard, Cooper & Gale, 415-646-4674, appearing for Defendant for American General Life Insurance;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Sandy Lim'S Second Motion To Compel Defendant American General Life Insurance Companys Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions

Plaintiff Sandy Lim's motion to compel Defendant American General Life Insurance Company's person most knowledgeable for deposition and for monetary sanctions is denied. American General, at Plaintiff's request, properly designated Angela Butler as the person most knowledgeable to testify on its behalf in response to Plaintiff's PMK notice. It did not refuse to make Ms. Butler available for deposition, but rather was forced to reschedule her deposition due to a personal family emergency on her part. The Court has reviewed in camera Ms. Butler's declaration, which substantiates Defendant's position.

It appears to the Court from the information submitted to it in camera that American General will not be in a position to make Ms. Butler available for a PMK deposition before April 26 at the earliest. American General should be prepared to address whether it intends to designate an alternative PMK witness and, if so, when that person's deposition may be taken in San Francisco, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.250(b).
In addition, the parties should be prepared to address whether any continuance of the existing summary judgment schedule and trial date will be required. Defendant American General Life Insurance Company shall designate a person most knowledgeable to testify on the remaining topics of the deposition notice and shall make that person available for deposition in San Francisco prior to May 31, 2019. Counsel shall meet and confer with respect to any other outstanding disputes regarding discovery requests appended to the PMK notice. Nicholas J. Boss, Esq. counsel for defendant American General Life Insurance Company is to submit an order that is apporved as to form and that complies with CRC 3.1312 by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant American General Life Insurance Company'S Motion For Protective Order

MINI MINUTES:
